capability: the GENERATE column of the operator cube realised at the spectral grain — INS (birth a reading online when a unit clears the void), SYN (merge a returning reading into its standing identity), REC (carry a learned reading as a prior) — completes the online, accumulating reader; recognize is the branch point and REC is the keystone that closes the loop
claim: exp-0003..0006 built the Differentiate and Relate operators on ρ (SEG=voidPeaks, DEF=readingCount, SIG=bornAssign, CON=coupling). The GENERATE column is what an ONLINE reader needs, and it is measurably harder: INS (online birth) loses the global spectrum that the batch DEF (readingCount) sees, so it births correctly only when the geography is separable and under-births on overlapping/short readings. SYN merges a returning reading into its identity (no re-birth churn). REC — a reading set learned from one stream carried as a prior into the next — recognises known readings with ZERO births and beats both the batch reader and cold online exactly where INS is weakest (short recurring blocks). REC is the return-arrow: the whole (a learned convention) sets the probability of the low.

predictions.INS_regime_dependent: on well-separated sequential readings the online INS+SYN reader matches or beats the batch reader (born ≈ true reads); on overlapping/short readings it under-births — INS cannot see the global spectrum
predictions.SYN_identity: returning readings are merged into their standing identity rather than re-born, so the online id count tracks the true reading count instead of inflating
predictions.REC_keystone: a prior learned from stream A, carried into stream B, births ZERO readings, is ≥ the batch reader, and beats cold online on the short-block regime — recognition replaces risky birth
predictions.why_generate_was_missing: the Generate operators are not an oversight but genuinely harder: Generate-online sacrifices the global view that Differentiate/Relate-batch enjoy, and only a prior (REC) restores it

verdict_tag: the Generate column is real; INS is regime-dependent, SYN gives clean identities, REC recognises with zero births and beats batch + cold on the hard regime — the keystone that closes the loop
control: recognize is a NEW pure export (src/core/spectral.js), composing bornAssign; every default engine path is byte-identical (node --test tests/*.test.js green). The regression lock is tests/recognize.test.js. The measure reads only each stream's units; boundaries/labels/tol are the held key; the REC prior is learned from a SEPARATE stream (A), never from the scored stream (B).
mechanism: online reader: recognize(u, readings, {floor=chance}) — a match is SYN (blend the unit into the reading's identity, sign-aware), a −1 is novelty that buffers toward an INS centroid birth after a short run. REC seeds `readings` with the lenses batch-learned from a paired stream, so recognize matches them from the first unit. batch = readingCount + bornAssign over global lenses.
disposition: recognize PROMOTED as an opt-in core primitive (the online reading branch point). The online INS+SYN reader and the REC prior-seeding live in the experiment as the demonstrated capability; INS is characterised (regime-dependent), REC confirmed as the keystone; not wired into any default reader. Parity preserved.
